# stalwart_dkim_signature (Resource)

Manages a DKIM signing key for a domain (the `DkimSignature` JMAP object).

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "stalwart_dkim_signature" "example" {
  domain_id   = stalwart_domain.example.id
  selector    = "tf2026"
  algorithm   = "ed25519-sha256" # or "rsa-sha256"
  private_key = var.dkim_private_key
  expiry      = "90d"
}

# Import an existing DKIM signature by its opaque id:
# terraform import stalwart_dkim_signature.example itxnfyrwaaaa
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `algorithm` (String) Signing algorithm: `ed25519-sha256` or `rsa-sha256`. Changing the algorithm replaces the signature.
- `private_key` (String, Sensitive) PEM-encoded private key used to sign outgoing messages.
- `selector` (String) Selector used to locate the DKIM public key in DNS.

### Optional

- `canonicalization` (String) Canonicalization algorithm. Defaults to `relaxed/relaxed`.
- `domain` (String) Name of the domain this object belongs to, e.g. `example.com`. Resolved to a domain id at apply time. Mutually exclusive with `domain_id`.
- `domain_id` (String) Opaque id of the domain this object belongs to. Mutually exclusive with `domain`.
- `expiry` (String) Duration after which this signature expires (e.g. `90d`), mapping to the `expire` field.
- `headers` (Set of String) Message headers to include in the DKIM signature. Defaults to a standard set.
- `report` (Boolean) Whether to request failure reports. Defaults to `true`.

### Read-Only

- `created_at` (String) Creation timestamp of the DKIM signature.
- `id` (String) Opaque server-assigned identifier of the DKIM signature.
- `public_key` (String) PEM-encoded public key derived from the private key (server-set).

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Import by opaque server-assigned id (DKIM signatures have no stable human-readable id):
terraform import stalwart_dkim_signature.example <opaque-id>
```
